Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants Daniel Maionchi And Dominic Maionchi; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Mar-19-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAMES HARVEY'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants Daniel Maionchi And Dominic Maionchi; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants.

Plaintiff James Ted Harvey's motion to compel depositions of Daniel Maionchi and Dominic Maionchi, and for sanctions, is continued for hearing to March 19, 2026. The parties are ordered to meet and confer telephonically or in person about deposition dates.

The court has reviewed the parties' meet and confer correspondence. Harvey's motion recites meet and confer efforts through December 12. The Maionchis offered dates in correspondence that came after December 16. Harvey says he filed the motion on December 16, but the file-stamp date is December 24 and the proof of service date is December 24. On the record in the moving papers, the court is persuaded this is a scheduling issue and not misuse of discovery. Regardless, Harvey is entitled to the depositions. The court directs the parties to meet and confer within 10 days by telephone or in person to agree to mutually convenient deposition dates. The court continues the matter for hearing to March 19, 2026. The parties may file simultaneous supplemental briefs no later than five court days before the continued hearing date to apprise the court of any matters still in dispute. The court does not order sanctions for a misuse of the discovery process to date but will consider whether sanctions are appropriate based on meet and confer efforts through the continued hearing date.
